commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cad654ad7765b302fcd137c9df2d53b3b79c2fec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Microsoft Teams Bot Framework webhook validation and attachment fetches so activity service URLs and media Authorization headers are explicitly scoped.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +112. Total +202 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main ignores the validator's serviceUrl parameter and only strips media Authorization after redirects, so the reported gaps are visible in code. No live Teams webhook or attachment flow was reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The maintainer-labeled auth change needs human approval on fail-closed serviceUrl compatibility and live Teams proof rather than an automated repair.

target.title: fix(msteams): bind bot framework service urls
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Microsoft Teams Bot Framework authentication so inbound tokens are bound to the activity `serviceUrl`, and keep media bearer tokens scoped to explicitly auth-allowlisted hosts.

## Changes

- Validate the Bot Framework JWT `serviceurl`/`serviceUrl` claim against the inbound Activity body `serviceUrl`.
- Parse the bounded webhook JSON body before full JWT validation while keeping the cheap Bearer pre-gate and payload-size handling.
- Strip media `Authorization` on initial and redirect fetches unless the target host is auth-allowlisted.
- Apply the auth-allowlist header helper to Bot Framework attachment metadata and view downloads.
- Extend Microsoft Teams monitor, SDK, and attachment tests for the new service URL and authorization behavior.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/attachments/bot-framework.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/attachments/shared.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/sdk.test.ts` - 4 test files passed, 110 tests passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` - passed.

Behavior addressed: Microsoft Teams Bot Framework requests are accepted only when the JWT service URL claim matches the Activity body, and media bearer tokens are sent only to auth-allowlisted hosts.
Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree with the repo Vitest wrapper.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/msteams/src/attachments/bot-framework.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/attachments/shared.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/sdk.test.ts`; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`.
Evidence after fix: Vitest reported 4 test files passed and 110 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: Microsoft Teams SDK, monitor, and attachment regression tests passed.
What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams/Bot Framework webhook traffic.

## Notes

- No live Microsoft Teams tenant, Bot Framework webhook, or production credentials were used.
